title: Wellspring: getMatches assembly was the highest-damage untested path after the ingest boundary (ADR-0003)
content: In grant-writing-nonprofits (Wellspring), after ADR-0001 covered the grants.gov normalization boundary, the next highest-blast-radius gap was getMatches (src/features/grant-ingest/scoring.ts:161) — the DB-backed entry point the dashboard MatchesTable renders. scoreGrant was unit-tested, but the assembly around it (the total>0 filter, fit-descending sort, limit, and IngestedGrant->GrantMatch mapping: Unknown-funder fallback, amount/date formatting, 80-char truncation) had zero tests. ADR-0003 (Accepted, 2026-05-27) adds getMatches.test.ts with 8 integration cases via the upsertGrants+beforeEach-clear pattern. No defect found — pure regression coverage. Pinned a deliberate inconsistency: getMatches passes ISO close dates through verbatim (only reformats MM/DD/YYYY) while deadline-radar parses both. Suite went 194->202. Lesson reinforced: when a pure core is tested but its DB-backed public wrapper that the UI consumes is not, the wrapper's filter/sort/format glue is the real regression surface — test it against the actual storage path, not just the pure function.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 08:52 UTC
Wellspring: APPROVE — grants.gov normalization hardening + getMatches assembly coverage (ADR-0003)
content: Reviewed the uncommitted changeset in grant-writing-nonprofits (Wellspring). The handoff described only ADR-0003/getMatches.test.ts and claimed 'no source change', but the working tree also carried real source edits in src/features/grant-ingest/grantsGov.ts: (1) stableHash() replaced Math.random() in the normalize() id fallback — correct, since ON CONFLICT(id) upserts depended on stable ids and the random fallback silently duplicated rows on every re-ingest of a payload missing both id and number; (2) toNum() now requires a digit in the cleaned string before Number(), closing the 'not a number' -> '' -> 0 hole that poisoned award-size scoring with phantom $0 awards. Both verified correct. getMatches/scoring.ts itself was unchanged. Verdict APPROVE: 0 critical/security/correctness findings; full suite 19 files/202 tests green, tsc clean, README version claims (Next 16/React 19/TS 6) verified accurate vs package.json. Standing Low note (pre-existing, non-gating): grant-ingest tests run against the REAL data/grants.db and DELETE FROM grants in beforeEach/afterAll — not hermetic; recommend an env-driven/:memory: test DB. Lesson: always reconcile the actual git changeset against the handoff narrative — the upstream agent under-reported the source diff.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 08:56 UTC
Grant Writing Nonprofits — baseline security scan findings (2026-05-27)
content: First security scan of grant-writing-nonprofits (Next.js 16 / React 19 / better-sqlite3). Findings: 0 Critical, 2 High, 1 Medium, 1 Low. H1 [OPEN]: live Gemini AIza API key in .env.local:2 — gitignored & never committed (verified git log -S), so working-tree-only exposure; rank High per secret err-higher. H2 [OPEN]: no auth layer — server actions run on singleton getActiveOrgId(); removeAttachment→deleteAttachment(id) has no org-ownership scoping = IDOR; intentional v0 ('v1 keys off session user') but blocking pre-deploy. M1 [OPEN]: npm audit 2 moderate, postcss XSS-in-stringify transitive via next (build-time). L1 [OPEN]: drafts/storage.ts:39,42 dynamic DDL (PRAGMA/ALTER) via interpolation but inputs hardcoded → latent only. Verified CLEAN: parameterized SQL everywhere, strong path-traversal defense in attachments/fs.ts + validation.ts, server-only key handling, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML, no console logging. Dedup signatures for next re-scan: .env.local:2#hardcoded-secret, actions.ts:127#missing-authz-idor, package.json#postcss-audit, drafts/storage.ts:42#dynamic-ddl.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 09:01 UTC
grant-writing-nonprofits — versioning convention & v0.2.0 release
content: Release decisions for grant-writing-nonprofits. KEY CONVENTION (from CHANGELOG header): project is PRE-1.0 and 'breaking platform changes increment the MINOR version' — do NOT bump MAJOR for breaking changes while <1.0. v0.2.0 [RELEASED 2026-05-27]: Next.js 14→16, React 18→19, Tailwind 3→4, TS 6, ESLint 10, Gemini SDK 0.24.x (commit 0c2f2a4). Classified MINOR (breaking platform migration under pre-1.0 rule). Bumped package.json 0.1.0→0.2.0; annotated tag v0.2.0 created on 0c2f2a4. CHANGELOG follows Keep a Changelog format. Prior baseline: 0.1.0 (initial scaffold). Note: changelog entries here may be pre-authored before the version file is bumped/tagged — always reconcile package.json + tag against CHANGELOG before assuming a release is complete.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 09:04 UTC
0.2.0 release event eventually fired with correct repo (grant-writing-nonprofits)
content: After two earlier runs where the release.version.bumped event referenced a wrong/unresolvable repo (ai-bookkeeper, commit 18fab0f), the event re-fired correctly referencing repo 'grant-writing-nonprofits', commit 0c2f2a4, version 0.2.0 — matching the registered codebase, with a v0.2.0 tag now present. Since 0c2f2a4 was already the last-processed commit (HEAD unchanged, 0 new commits), the outcome was 'docs already current' with no edits. Takeaway: a re-fired release event for an already-synced commit is a no-op; confirm HEAD==last-processed and report current rather than re-syncing.
